Schwartz) is associated with Steven Paul Plotnick as a doctoral advisor; Steven Plotnick is a professor of mathematics. This indicates the Wikidata entity is likely a mathematician or academic in a scientific field, which does not align with the career of the architect described on the card.

results.possibleIdentity: Kenneth Schwartz is an architect who was active in the late 1960s and 1970s. He graduated from the Rhode Island School of Design (RISD) in 1970 with a Bachelor of Architecture and a Bachelor of Fine Arts in Architecture. During his early career, he was based in Waterbury, Connecticut, residing at 135 Farmington Avenue. Schwartz was associated with the Institute of Applied Technology (IAT) of the National Bureau of Standards (NBS), which in the late 1960s and early 1970s was heavily involved in building research and technical evaluation for federal housing programs such as HUD's 'Operation Breakthrough'. His professional focus was on architecture and building systems. His association with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) likely stems from the organization's Technical Services program, which maintained a network of thousands of engineers, scientists, and technical professionals to consult with artists on collaborative projects. In later years, he established the architectural firm Kenneth Schwartz & Associates in Waterbury, Connecticut.
